content: p We haven't had a new film from Gore Verbinski for nine years. But the director who brought us the first three Pirates of the Caribbean movies, the nightmare-inducing horror of  em The Ring  em   2002 , and the Oscar-winning hijinks of  em Rango  em   2011  is back in peak form with  a href    Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die.  em   a  It's a darkly satirical, inventive, and hugely entertaining time-loop adventure that also serves as a cautionary tale about our widespread online technology addiction.  p   p  strong  Some spoilers below but no major reveals.   strong   p   p Sam Rockwell stars as an otherwise unnamed man who shows up at a  a href    diner  a  in Los Angeles looking like a homeless person but claiming to be a time traveler from an apocalyptic future. He s there to recruit the locals into his war against a rogue AI, although the diner patrons are understandably dubious about his sanity.   I come from a nightmare apocalypse,  he assures the crowd about his grubby appearance.  This is the height of f  ing fashion!    p  p  a href    full article  a   p   p  a href
